claim: Agent Client Protocol (ACP) standardizes agent-to-client communication, reducing duplicate work across different editor/IDE plugins
actionable: Use ACP as the standard protocol for agent-client communication to avoid building separate integrations for each IDE/editor/platform
source_title: Scaling Agents on Kubernetes with acpx and ACP — Onur Solmaz, OpenClaw

claim: Workflow engines for agents can automate repetitive evaluation tasks like PR review, conflict resolution, and shallow refactoring without creating poor-quality output
actionable: Build agent workflow engines for mechanical tasks like PR validation, conflict resolution, and shallow bug fixes; reserve human review for design decisions
source_title: Scaling Agents on Kubernetes with acpx and ACP — Onur Solmaz, OpenClaw

claim: Disposable, per-task agent instances on Kubernetes (one agent per task) with synchronized file state is the emerging pattern for enterprise agent systems
actionable: For high-volume agent tasks, provision isolated Kubernetes pods per task with persistent state synchronization rather than long-lived agent instances
source_title: Scaling Agents on Kubernetes with acpx and ACP — Onur Solmaz, OpenClaw

claim: Giving agents full system access (full IDE/computer environment) makes them significantly more powerful than constrained tool-only systems
actionable: When designing agentic systems, prioritize full environment access (terminal, file system, IDE) over tool APIs alone for maximum capability
source_title: Scaling Agents on Kubernetes with acpx and ACP — Onur Solmaz, OpenClaw
